Allen Vision Lab

The Allen Vision Lab studies grounded machine perception, building research graphs that connect researchers, papers, and the institutions behind them. We focus on trustworthy computer vision, provenance-aware data pipelines, and human-centered evaluation of perception systems. Our work emphasizes reproducibility and the careful documentation of the evidence behind every published claim, so that results can be independently verified.

The lab is directed by Professor Ada Lovelace and brings together students and staff across the Paul G. Allen School. Current members include Ada Lovelace, Alan Turing, and Grace Hopper, who collaborate on shared datasets and open-source tooling for the broader research community. The group meets weekly to review experiments and discuss new directions.

Members

  • Ada Lovelace — Principal Investigator
  • Alan Turing — Postdoctoral Researcher
  • Grace Hopper — PhD Student

Research Areas

Computer vision, knowledge graphs, and reproducible machine learning are the core research areas pursued by the lab, alongside long-term efforts in provenance tracking and grounded AI descriptions for scholarly knowledge graphs.
